name: Skin Cycling
description: A skincare routine method that rotates active ingredients (retinol, exfoliants) with recovery nights to minimize irritation and maximize efficacy. Popularized on TikTok and endorsed by dermatologists.

name: Glass Skin
description: K-Beauty trend focused on achieving an ultra-luminous, poreless, perfectly hydrated complexion. Involves layering lightweight hydrating products including essences, serums, and ampoules.

name: Niacinamide Serums
description: Vitamin B3 derivative that addresses multiple skin concerns simultaneously: minimizes pores, evens skin tone, reduces fine lines, controls sebum, and strengthens the skin barrier. Widely formulated in concentrations of 5-10%.

name: Slugging
description: Applying a thick layer of petrolatum or occlusive balm as the final step in a nighttime skincare routine to lock in moisture and accelerate skin barrier repair. Popular in Korean skincare and trending globally.

name: LED Light Therapy Masks
description: At-home LED devices using red light (collagen stimulation), blue light (acne-fighting), and near-infrared light (deep tissue repair). Professional-grade results now accessible to consumers through brands like Omnilux and CurrentBody.

name: Retinol Alternatives — Bakuchiol
description: Plant-derived retinol alternative from Psoralea corylifolia seeds. Clinically proven to reduce fine lines and improve skin tone with minimal irritation, suitable for sensitive skin and during pregnancy.
tags: クリーンビューティー

name: Scalp Care Revolution
description: The skincare-ification of scalp treatment. Serums, exfoliants, and SPF products designed specifically for the scalp. Addresses dandruff, hair loss, oil balance, and scalp microbiome health.

name: Peptide Skincare
description: Short chains of amino acids that signal skin to produce more collagen and elastin. New generation peptides target specific receptors for anti-aging, skin repair, and barrier strengthening with clinical efficacy data.

name: Barrier Repair Skincare
description: Products focused on restoring the stratum corneum using ceramides, fatty acids, and cholesterol in ratios matching the skin's natural composition. Key for eczema, rosacea, and over-exfoliated skin recovery.

name: AI-Powered Skin Analysis
description: Smartphone apps and in-store devices using computer vision and AI to analyze skin conditions, pore size, pigmentation, and wrinkles. Brands like L'Oréal and Perfect Corp offer personalized product recommendations based on facial scans.

name: Tranexamic Acid for Hyperpigmentation
description: Originally a medical hemostatic agent, now widely adopted in skincare for its ability to interrupt melanin production pathways. More tolerable than hydroquinone with comparable brightening results.

name: Waterless Beauty
description: Products formulated without water as a primary ingredient, using plant extracts, oils, and butters as the base. Higher concentration of active ingredients, longer shelf life without preservatives, and reduced environmental footprint.
tags: クリーンビューティー

name: Microcurrent Facial Devices
description: At-home devices delivering low-level electrical currents to stimulate facial muscles and boost ATP production. Brands like NuFACE and ZIIP offer results comparable to professional facial treatments for lifting and toning.

name: Azelaic Acid Skincare
description: Dicarboxylic acid derived from grains with multi-functional benefits: anti-inflammatory for rosacea, antibacterial for acne, and melanin-inhibiting for hyperpigmentation. Safe for all skin types and pregnancy.

name: Upcycled Beauty Ingredients
description: Cosmetic formulations using byproducts from food and beverage industries: coffee grounds, grape seeds, spent vanilla, and fruit peels. Rich in antioxidants while reducing agricultural waste.
tags: クリーンビューティー

name: Probiotic Skincare
description: Skincare products containing live beneficial bacteria or bacterial lysates to balance the skin microbiome, reduce inflammation, and strengthen barrier function. Growing evidence supports microbiome-focused formulations for sensitive and acne-prone skin.

name: Sunscreen Innovation 2025
description: New generation UV filters including Tinosorb M and S offering broad-spectrum protection with improved aesthetics. Mineral-chemical hybrid formulas, tinted SPFs with skincare benefits, and photostable formulations that don't degrade in sunlight.

name: Gua Sha and Facial Massage Tools
description: Traditional Chinese medicine tool experiencing mainstream beauty revival. Jade and rose quartz tools used for lymphatic drainage, reducing puffiness, and releasing facial tension. Dermatologist-approved for improving circulation.

name: Bonding Hair Treatments
description: Chemistry-based hair treatments that repair broken disulfide bonds in the hair cortex. Olaplex pioneered the category; now joined by K18, Redken Acidic Bonding, and Schwarzkopf Bond Enforcing for chemical-damaged hair restoration.

name: Skin Microbiome Testing
description: Consumer DNA-sequencing kits and professional lab tests that analyze the bacterial ecosystem on skin. Results used to personalize probiotic skincare routines, identify dysbiosis patterns, and address chronic skin conditions.

name: Cushion Foundation Technology
description: Korean-originated applicator system delivering buildable coverage with skincare benefits. Modern formulations include SPF, hyaluronic acid, and long-wear technology. Expanding beyond K-beauty into global luxury brands.

name: Body Care Premiumization
description: Consumers applying facial skincare standards to body products. Exfoliating body serums, targeted treatments for body acne and stretch marks, and luxury body oils with active ingredients like retinol and vitamin C for below-the-neck skincare.

name: Lip Blushing and Semi-Permanent Makeup
description: Cosmetic tattooing technique adding natural-looking color and definition to lips with 2-3 year longevity. Part of the semi-permanent makeup revolution including microblading, powder brows, and lash enhancement.
tags: セミパーマネントメイク

name: Vitamin C Serums — New Stable Derivatives
description: Beyond ascorbic acid: new stable vitamin C derivatives including ascorbyl glucoside, 3-O-ethyl ascorbic acid, and THD ascorbate offering oxidation resistance with comparable brightening efficacy. Solve the shelf-stability problem plaguing pure vitamin C.

name: Refillable Beauty Packaging
description: Luxury and mass-market brands adopting refillable cartridge systems for fragrance, skincare, and makeup. Chanel, Hourglass, and Charlotte Tilbury lead the movement reducing packaging waste by up to 80% per refill purchase.

name: Retinol in Body Care
description: Extension of the retinol boom beyond facial skincare to address body aging signs: keratosis pilaris, rough elbows, body acne, and stretch marks. Lower concentrations than face products ensure tolerability for larger surface areas.

name: Fermented Skincare
description: Fermentation process enhances bioavailability of active ingredients and creates new beneficial compounds. Galactomyces ferment filtrate, bifida ferment lysate, and saccharomyces ferment are powerhouse ingredients in K-beauty formulations.

name: Fragrance Layering
description: Technique of combining multiple fragrances to create a personalized signature scent. Niche perfume houses now offering layering guides and complementary collections. Body lotion, oil, and parfum coordination for all-day scent longevity.

name: Invisible Acne Patches
description: Ultra-thin hydrocolloid patches that absorb pus and sebum overnight while protecting blemishes. Advanced versions include salicylic acid, tea tree oil, and microneedle technology for deeper penetration. Daytime-wear innovations with makeup compatibility.

name: Hyaluronic Acid — Molecular Weight Science
description: Understanding how different molecular weights of hyaluronic acid work at different skin depths. High molecular weight plumps the surface; low molecular weight penetrates deeper for structural hydration. Multi-weight formulas offer comprehensive moisture.

name: Tone-Up Sunscreen
description: Korean innovation combining SPF protection with optical brightening pigments that instantly neutralize dullness and sallowness. Available in various tones to correct different undertones. Replaced traditional tinted moisturizers for daily UV protection.

name: Postbiotic Skincare
description: Inactivated microorganisms and their metabolites used in skincare for stability advantages over live probiotics. Bacterial ferment filtrates provide anti-inflammatory, soothing, and barrier-enhancing benefits without shelf-stability challenges.

name: Hair Lamination Treatments
description: Salon treatment coating each hair strand with a protective film of plant-based proteins, sealing the cuticle for extreme shine and smoothness lasting 4-6 weeks. Different from Keratin: no formaldehyde, safe for color-treated hair.
tags: サロントリートメント

name: Personalized Beauty AI Apps
description: Apps using machine learning to analyze face shape, skin undertone, and lifestyle factors to recommend personalized makeup shades, skincare routines, and haircolor. Perfect Corp's YouCam and L'Oréal's Modiface lead the segment.

name: Bromelain and Enzyme Exfoliants
description: Fruit-derived proteolytic enzymes (papain from papaya, bromelain from pineapple) that digest dead skin proteins without the sensitization risk of AHAs/BHAs. Ideal for sensitive skin seeking gentle chemical exfoliation.
tags: エクスフォリエーション

name: Lymphatic Drainage Beauty Devices
description: At-home devices using vibration, suction, or gentle pressure to stimulate lymphatic drainage and reduce facial puffiness. Popularized by gua sha, now expanded to electronic devices from brands like Foreo and NuFACE.

name: Blue Light Protection Skincare
description: Products incorporating antioxidants and physical blockers to defend against high-energy visible (HEV) blue light from screens. Iron oxides in tinted sunscreens offer meaningful protection. Debate continues on actual damage levels from indoor screens.

name: Multifunction Beauty Sticks
description: Solid stick products for cheeks, lips, and eyes in one product for minimal beauty routines. Hybrid formulas blending skincare and color cosmetics. Fast application for a 'your skin but better' aesthetic dominating Gen Z beauty.

name: Adaptogens in Skincare
description: Plant extracts traditionally used in herbal medicine now formulated into skincare: ashwagandha, rhodiola, reishi mushroom, and holy basil. Claims center on anti-stress benefits for skin, reducing cortisol-induced inflammation and oxidative damage.

name: Hair Gloss Treatments
description: Semi-permanent treatment depositing tone and shine without the commitment of permanent color. Clear glosses enhance natural hair's luminosity; tinted versions refresh faded color. At-home versions from brands like Kristin Ess democratize salon results.

name: Squalane in Skincare
description: Stabilized form of squalene (naturally in human sebum) derived from sugarcane or olive oil. Lightweight, non-comedogenic oil that mimics skin's natural lipids for superior absorption. Safe for all skin types including acne-prone.

name: Makeup for Mature Skin
description: Growing category addressing specific needs of skin 40+: thinner consistency foundations that don't settle into lines, blurring primers, and hydrating-first formulations. Brands like Charlotte Tilbury and ILIA now offer dedicated mature skin ranges.

name: At-Home Radiofrequency Devices
description: Consumer-grade RF devices delivering controlled heat to stimulate fibroblast activity and collagen neogenesis. Brands like Tripollar and Newa offer clinical results for skin tightening and wrinkle reduction with weekly home treatments.

name: Centella Asiatica (Cica) Skincare
description: Traditional healing herb with clinically validated wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and barrier repair properties. Key active compounds: asiaticoside, madecassoside, and asiatic acid. Cornerstone of sensitive skin and post-procedure recovery routines.

name: Clean Fragrance Movement
description: Transparent fragrance formulations avoiding synthetic musks, phthalates, and known allergens. Brands like Skylar, Phlur, and Ellis Brooklyn offer full ingredient disclosure. Driving demand for natural isolates and sustainably sourced materials.
tags: クリーンビューティー

name: Collagen-Boosting Skincare Science
description: Beyond topical collagen (too large to penetrate): ingredients clinically proven to stimulate fibroblasts — retinoids, vitamin C, copper peptides, and growth factors. Distinction between actual collagen synthesis vs. surface-level plumping.

name: Underarm and Intimate Skincare
description: Premium skincare extending to underarms, bikini line, and intimate areas. Dedicated deodorants with skin-brightening actives, exfoliating solutions for ingrowns, and pH-balanced intimate wash formulations. Fastest-growing personal care subcategory.

name: Biodegradable Glitter and Eco Makeup
description: Plant cellulose-based glitter alternatives biodegrading within weeks vs. plastic microplastic versions persisting centuries. Cosmetology schools and festivals adopting eco-glitter. Driving broader conversation about microplastics in beauty formulations.

name: Snowmushroom (Tremella) Hydration
description: Tremella fuciformis polysaccharide derived from white snow mushrooms holding up to 500x its weight in water — outperforming hyaluronic acid. Creates flexible moisture film adapting to humidity levels. Star ingredient in Gen Z clean beauty.

name: Dermaplaning at Home
description: Controlled exfoliation removing dead skin cells and vellus hair using a surgical-grade blade or consumer-safe device. Instantly improves skin texture and product absorption. At-home tools from Dermaflash and Tinkle make the technique accessible.
tags: エクスフォリエーション
